message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
Implement a minimalistic, monospace design that's customizable and reusable across pages

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me explore the project structure first.

input.prompt: Explore the project at /home/resu/src/incomplete.fun. I need to understand: the full directory structure, key config files (package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.js or similar), any existing layout/component files, and current styling approach (CSS, Tailwind, etc). Read the main layout file and a sample page. Report file paths, tech stack, and current styling setup. Search breadth: medium.

content.text:
Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Project Exploration Summary: incomplete.fun

### Directory Structure
```
/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/
├── site/                          # Main Next.js application
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx             # Root layout component
│   │   ├── page.tsx               # Home page
│   │   ├── globals.css            # Global styles
│   │   └── fib/[n]/page.tsx       # Dynamic fibonacci page
│   ├── package.json
│   ├── tsconfig.json
│   ├── next.config.ts
│   ├── postcss.config.mjs
│   ├── bun.lock
│   ├── .gitignore
│   ├── next-env.d.ts
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   ├── AGENTS.md
│   └── node_modules/
├── .git/
├── .claude/
├── .codex/
├── .entire/
└── .devenv/
```

### Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 16.2.5 (latest version with breaking changes)
- **React**: 19.2.4
- **TypeScript**: 5.x
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4.x + PostCSS with @tailwindcss/postcss
- **Package Manager**: Bun (evidenced by bun.lock file)
- **Deployment/Analytics**: Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights

### Key Configuration Files

**package.json** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/package.json):
- Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `start`
- Tailwind CSS 4 configured as dev dependency
- PostCSS integration via @tailwindcss/postcss
- Minimal dependencies (react, react-dom, next, vercel analytics)

**tsconfig.json** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/tsconfig.json):
- Target: ES2020
- Strict mode enabled
- Module resolution: bundler
- Path alias: `@/*` → root level
- JSX: react-jsx

**next.config.ts** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/next.config.ts):
- Minimal config, mostly empty placeholder

**postcss.config.mjs** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/postcss.config.mjs):
- Single plugin: `@tailwindcss/postcss`

### Styling Approach
- **CSS Framework**: Tailwind CSS v4 (modern version using @tailwindcss/postcss)
- **Global Styles**: `/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/globals.css` (single line: `@import "tailwindcss";`)
- **Component Styling**: Currently using inline styles via `style` prop (not Tailwind classes)
- **No Tailwind Config File**: No tailwind.config.* file present; using defaults

### Layout & Pages

**Root Layout** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/layout.tsx):
- Metadata setup (title: "incomplete.fun", description provided)
- Imports globals.css for styling
- Includes Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights components
- Simple HTML structure: html > body > {children}
- No styling applied to root elements

**Home Page** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/page.tsx):
- Simple component with monospace font and 2rem padding
- Lists page routes (currently only Fibonacci link to `/fib/10`)
- Uses inline styles, no Tailwind classes

**Sample Dynamic Page** (/home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/fib/[n]/page.tsx):
- Server component with async params handling
- Implements Fibonacci calculation using TypeScript bigint
- Input validation for non-negative integers
- Similar styling: monospace font with padding via inline styles
- Uses `PageProps<'/fib/[n]'>` type annotation (Next.js 16 specific syntax)

### Current Styling Observations
- Tailwind CSS is configured but **not actively used** in current components
- All styling relies on inline `style` props with basic CSS properties
- No Tailwind utility classes present in existing pages
- Setup ready for Tailwind adoption (PostCSS pipeline is complete)
- Minimal and semantic HTML structure

lastPrompt: Implement a minimalistic, monospace design that's customizable and reusable across pages

content.text: Here's my plan: create a CSS design system in `globals.css` with custom properties (colors, font, spacing), a reusable `PageLayout` component that pages wrap themselves in (accepting an optional `accent` color override), and update both existing pages to use it.

content.content: The file /home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/globals.css has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +  --mono: ui-monospace, 'Cascadia Code', 'Source Code Pro', Menlo, Consolas, 'DejaVu Sans Mono', monospace;

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/components/page-layout.tsx (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
The file /home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/page.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
The file /home/resu/src/incomplete.fun/site/app/fib/[n]/page.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: Invalid input: {raw}. Please provide a non-negative integer.

content.text:
Done. Here's what was implemented:

**`globals.css`** — CSS custom properties define the entire design system:
- `--mono` font stack, `--bg`/`--fg`/`--accent`/`--muted`/`--border` colors, `--pad`/`--max-width` layout
- Automatic dark mode via `prefers-color-scheme`
- Base element styles (links, headings, code, lists) so pages just use semantic HTML

**`app/components/page-layout.tsx`** — one reusable wrapper component:
- Applies `.page` (padding + max-width)
- Accepts optional `accent` prop to override `--accent` per-page, e.g.:
  ```tsx
  <PageLayout accent="#cc5500">…</PageLayout>
  ```

**Both pages** updated to drop the inline `style` props and use `<PageLayout>`.

To customize the theme globally, edit the CSS variables in `:root`. To customize per-page, pass an `accent` prop or extend `PageLayout` with more CSS variable overrides.
